puzzle.title: The Message Amidst the Mockery
puzzle.intro: For centuries, a devoted servant of Allah delivered a message of warning to a stubborn and arrogant people. Despite tireless efforts and patient pleas, their hearts remained sealed in disbelief. This individual's unwavering faith would ultimately lead to a world-altering event, a testament to divine justice and mercy for the few who heeded the call. Can you uncover the identity of this steadfast messenger?
clues: I called to my people night and day, but my call only increased their flight from the truth.
They would put their fingers in their ears and cover themselves with their garments, persisting in their arrogance.
I lived among them for a lifetime, a total of 950 years, yet most did not believe.
I pleaded with my Lord for my own child, who refused to join me, but was told they were not of my family due to their unrighteous actions.
Under Allah's watchful eyes and inspiration, I constructed a great vessel, a means of salvation from a coming deluge.
The floodwaters engulfed the entire earth, and only those who boarded the Ark with me, along with a pair of every creature, were saved.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Hud
Prophet Salih
Prophet Nuh
Prophet Shu'ayb
Prophet Lut

trial.label: What Was My Great Trial?
options: Being cast into a fire by a tyrannical king
Calling my people to Tawhid for 950 years and the Great Flood
Facing the magicians of a powerful Pharaoh
A test involving the sacrifice of my son
Being swallowed by a giant fish
trial.answer: Calling my people to Tawhid for 950 years and the Great Flood

location.label: What Was the Key Element of Salvation?
options: A staff that turned into a serpent
A she-camel that emerged from a rock
A coat that inspired a ruler's dream
The Ark (Al-Fulk)
A flock of birds carrying baked clay stones

outcome.label: What Was the Ultimate Outcome?
options: My people were destroyed by a violent, roaring wind
My family was saved, but my wife turned back and was destroyed with the wicked cities
My entire nation accepted faith, and their punishment was averted
The disbelievers, including my son and wife, were drowned, and a new civilization began with the believers.
I was raised to the heavens, and my people were left in dispute
outcome.answer: The disbelievers, including my son and wife, were drowned, and a new civilization began with the believers.

puzzle.arabic: وَقَالَ نُوحٌۭ رَّبِّ لَا تَذَرْ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ دَيَّارًا
puzzle.verse: And Noah said, "My Lord, do not leave upon the earth from among the disbelievers an inhabitant. (71:26)
